title: A look at Unbroken - A solo, survival, resource management, dungeon crawl
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lN4Nv7R4sBI

Daniel (@dungeondive) examines **Unbroken**, Artem Safarov's solo game of survival and revenge — carefully setting aside the game's troubled Kickstarter fulfilment to focus on the game itself. The setup is pure pulp: your party ambushed and slaughtered, you're left for dead and claw your way back out of the dark for revenge. "Die Hard in a dungeon," the box promises; "if Tarantino made a dungeon crawl," Daniel offers.

He places Unbroken at the tactical end of a spectrum that runs from the pure luck of Mini Rogue through Iron Helm and Rogue Dungeon — this is tight, almost euro-style cube-pushing resource management across four levels and four fights, with weapon-crafting upgrade trees, class powers, skills and a punishing economy that leaves little room for error. It's very hard; he admits he's never won on normal. What he praises without reservation is the presentation: gorgeous components, deeply fleshed-out world and backstory, and documentation — flowcharts, quick-start cards, a glossary — that he ranks alongside Sleeping Gods. A demanding optimisation puzzle wrapped in a superb dark-fantasy skin.

----

Unbroken rewards near-perfect optimisation with very little slack for mistakes. Do you love that kind of tight, solvable challenge, or do you prefer a solo game with more room for luck and improvisation?
